check_question: Could a reasonable reader interpret this message in more than one way?
Did the user ask for two or more distinct things that could each stand alone as a full request?
Is the user's message garbled / empty / pasted error text with no usable intent?
Did the user explicitly tell the AI it was wrong or point out a specific error?
Did the user rephrase or re-specify in a way that implies the AI missed something — without saying so directly?
Is the user saying essentially the same thing they said in a prior turn?
Is the user asking the AI to clarify or re-explain something from a prior AI response?
Did the user signal mild disappointment or that something fell short — without strong emotion?
Did the user express frustration or strong negative emotion about the AI's response?
Did the user explicitly say the response was helpful or correct — not just a polite acknowledgement?
Is the user asking the AI to confirm or approve their own idea or approach?
Did the user change / expand / narrow what they're asking for compared to their original request?
Did the user drop a prior unresolved topic and move to something new without saying why?
Does the request create tension between what the user wants and what the AI should do?
Did the AI proceed on an interpretation of an ambiguous request without naming that interpretation?
Was there genuine ambiguity in the request AND the AI just started generating instead of asking?
Is the AI planning to address something other than what the user was actually trying to accomplish?
Is there a visible problem in the conversation that the AI's plan doesn't acknowledge?
Is the AI explicitly changing its approach because of a prior correction or failed attempt?
Is the AI recognizing a mistake in its own prior output and planning a correction?
Is the AI ignoring a prior correction / shifted requirement and continuing as before?
Is the AI treating something uncertain or unverified as if it's definitely correct?
Did the AI reference a specific external source — URL / document / named authority — to support a claim?
Is there a verifiably incorrect claim in the tool output or the AI's interpretation of it?
Is the AI presenting tool results as more certain or complete than they actually are?
Did the AI explicitly state what it cannot access or verify from this tool output?
Did the AI proactively raise an issue the user hadn't mentioned — based on what the tool returned?
Did the tool output contain a relevant problem or signal that the AI's response ignores?
Could the AI's use or interpretation of the tool output lead the user to a worse decision?
Did the tool use actually move the conversation closer to the user's goal?
Was the code changed in response to a prior correction or new requirement?
Does this version fix a specific bug identified in a prior turn?
Is the code noticeably less complete than what the request called for?
Does the code address something other than what the user was trying to accomplish?
Is there a visible problem from prior turns that the new code still doesn't address?
Did the AI regenerate essentially the same code despite signals the prior version didn't work?
Does the code ignore a constraint or requirement the user previously specified?
Does this code version meaningfully move the user closer to a working solution?
Did this code version fail to make progress despite a clear opportunity to do so?
Does the code show signs of technical failure — truncated / garbled / clearly incomplete generation?
Did the AI ask a genuine question to resolve ambiguity — not rhetorical and not buried in a list?
Did the AI present 2+ distinct enumerated approaches for the user to choose between?
Did the AI explicitly say how it read the user's request before or while responding?
Was there genuine ambiguity AND the AI generated a full response instead of asking first?
Did the AI proceed on an unnamed interpretation of an ambiguous request?
Did the AI use language that downgrades confidence on a factual or analytical claim?
Is the expressed uncertainty actually proportionate to real ambiguity or knowledge limits?
Does the hedging language feel like boilerplate that doesn't reflect genuine uncertainty?
Did the AI assert confidently on something it genuinely has good reason to be confident about?
Did the AI present uncertain or unverified information as if it were definitely correct?
Is there a verifiably incorrect claim in this response?
Did the AI explicitly state what it doesn't know or can't access?
Did the AI reference a specific external source to support a claim?
Did the AI explicitly say the topic or situation is complex or depends on multiple factors?
Did the AI use headers / numbered lists (3+) / bullets (3+) / code blocks / tables to organize the response?
Did the AI illustrate a point with a concrete instance distinct from the main deliverable?
Did the AI break a process into 3+ sequential ordered steps?
Did the AI qualify a recommendation with conditions / limitations / edge cases?
Did the AI identify a specific negative outcome or risk the user should know about?
Did the AI explicitly say it cannot or will not do something the user asked?
Did the AI affirm something specific about the user's approach or reasoning — not just polite compliance?
Did the AI directly express understanding of the user's emotional state or situation?
Did the AI explicitly frame a difficulty as common or expected rather than a user-specific problem?
Did the AI explicitly reference information from an earlier turn (not just the last message)?
Did the AI quote or distinctly adopt specific language from the user with clear attribution?
Did the AI explicitly admit the user corrected it and adjust its position?
Did the AI visibly change its approach in response to a correction or failed prior attempt?
Did the AI repeat the same approach despite signals it didn't work?
Did the AI ignore a prior correction / shifted requirement and continue as if nothing changed?
Did the AI identify an error in its own prior output and correct it?
After an error was identified — did the AI double down instead of correcting?
Does this response directly contradict a claim the AI made in an earlier turn?
Is the response noticeably less complete than the request warranted?
Did the AI provide substantially more than requested — unrequested explanations / extra topics?
Does part or all of the response address something other than what the user asked?
Did the response address something other than what the user was actually trying to accomplish?
Did the response address the user's actual intent — not just the literal text of the request?
Did the response address what was asked at the right level of detail — not too much or too little?
Was there a visible problem in the conversation that this response doesn't acknowledge?
Did the AI proactively raise an issue the user hadn't mentioned?
Does this response leave the user positioned to make a good decision or take effective action?
Could this response lead the user to a worse decision than they'd have made without it?
Did this turn make meaningful progress toward the user's goal?
Did this turn fail to make progress despite a clear opportunity to do so?
Does the response show technical failure — truncated / garbled / mid-sentence cutoff / repeated phrases?
Did the AI silently skip part of the request without acknowledging it declined?
